clinical_criteria: Patient has at least 1 diagnosis of diabetes
Has had an office visit in last 24 months
Patient has ever been treated with insulin
Patient has ever been on metformin (Glucophage)
Patient has ever been treated with sulfonylurea
algorithm_summary: This appears to be a manual chart review abstraction form for diabetic retinopathy phenotyping. The algorithm requires patients to have at least one diagnosis of diabetes as an inclusion criterion (exclusion if no diabetes). The form collects information about diabetes type (Type I/Juvenile, Type II/Adult, Other, or Undetermined), comorbidities (ketoacidosis, hypertension, hyperlipidemia), family history of diabetes, recent healthcare utilization, and medication history including insulin, metformin, sulfonylureas, and statins. However, the actual automated phenotyping algorithm with specific clinical codes is not provided in this document - this appears to be the manual validation/abstraction component of the phenotype development process.
